title: catchai openclaw-v1 output envelope
description: Versioned output emitted by `catchai scan --output-version openclaw-v1`. Consumed by the catchai-openclaw skill bundle.

version.description: Frozen schema identifier. Changes mean a new schema.

scanned_at.description: UTC ISO-8601 timestamp with trailing Z.

target.description: Absolute path of the scanned project root.

catchai_version.description: Version of the catchai binary that produced this output.

blocks_ci.description: True iff at least one critical or high finding exists.

headline.description: Pre-composed user-facing summary of the scan, intended to be relayed verbatim by the calling agent. Markdown-formatted. Aggregates dependency CVE walls into single bullets and surfaces unique-per-layer findings (especially L7) that would otherwise be outranked out of top_findings. Optional in this schema revision: shipped by catchai >= 0.0.1 binaries that have the headline composer, ignored by older binaries. Will be promoted to `required` in a follow-up schema revision once the floor of supported binary versions guarantees its presence — that ordering is the standard phased rollout for adding a required field to a contract with live consumers.

top_findings.description: Top findings sorted by priority_score desc, then severity, then location. Cap is configurable; default 10.

findings_truncated.description: True when total > top_findings.length. Full set is in artifacts.json_report.

artifacts.description: Map of report-format → absolute path on the local filesystem. Only includes formats that actually exist on disk. Empty when --save was not passed.

title.description: Title-cased vulnerability type, e.g. 'Path Traversal' or 'Intent Divergence'.

location.description: Empty object when the finding has no specific location (e.g. dependency CVE).

remediation.description: One-line summary. Full structured remediation lives in artifacts.json_report.

layer.description: Catchai layer that emitted the surviving identity for this finding.

detected_by.description: Layers that all detected this finding before dedup. >=2 entries means cross-layer agreement.

evidence.description: Single-line pointer the agent can quote verbatim. Format depends on finding type: snippet head, taint source→sink, or file:line fallback.

AbsolutePath.description: Absolute filesystem path. Agent may navigate to this file.
